**Acceptance**: All tasks closed, tests pass, 100% coverage maintained

title: US3-A: D1 galaxy schema migration
description: Create migrations/0002_galaxy_schema.sql with star_systems, routes, and trade_pairs tables per data-model.md. Includes all indexes (bidirectional route lookup, trade pair lookup, name prefix search). UNIQUE constraints serve as double-seeding guard. Skills: /d1-repository-implementation, /cloudflare-migrations. Acceptance: Migration file applies cleanly to empty D1; all three tables created with correct columns and indexes.

title: US3-B: Galaxy seeder project scaffolding
description: Create tools/galaxy-seeder/ with package.json (tsx dependency), tsconfig.json (Node.js target, ES2022), following tools/galaxy-generator/ conventions. Skills: /prefactoring. Acceptance: Project compiles; tsx can execute entry point.

title: US3-C: Seeder file reader (reader.ts)
description: Implement tools/galaxy-seeder/src/reader.ts to read metadata.json, systems/*.json, routes.json from generator output directory. Uses fs/promises with concurrency limiting (MAX_CONCURRENCY = 100 per galaxy-generator pattern). TDD with vi.mock('node:fs/promises'). Skills: /typescript-unit-testing. Acceptance: Reads all system files and routes from fixture directory; respects concurrency limit; returns typed data structures.

title: US3-D: Seeder input validator (validator.ts)
description: Implement tools/galaxy-seeder/src/validator.ts to validate: metadata exists, routes exist, all route IDs reference real systems, no duplicate system IDs/coordinates, required fields present, economics.worldTradeNumber is numeric for all systems. TDD. Skills: /typescript-unit-testing, /error-handling-patterns. Acceptance: Valid data passes; missing metadata fails; route referencing non-existent system fails; system missing worldTradeNumber fails with descriptive error.

title: US3-E: Route graph and BFS (graph.ts)
description: Implement tools/galaxy-seeder/src/graph.ts to build adjacency list from routes and perform BFS to depth 5. For each system with routes, BFS discovers all reachable pairs within 5 hops. Deduplicates pairs (systemA < systemB lexicographic ordering), keeping minimum hop count. Only Oikumene systems (~250) have routes. TDD with small fixture graphs. Skills: /typescript-unit-testing. Acceptance: BFS from hub finds all reachable pairs within 5 hops; pairs deduplicated with min hops; isolated systems produce zero pairs.

title: US3-F: BTN computation (btn.ts)
description: Implement tools/galaxy-seeder/src/btn.ts with distanceModifier(hops) and computeBtn(wtnA, wtnB, hops) functions per GURPS Far Trader formula. Distance modifier: hops<=1→0, hops<=2→0.5, hops 3-5→1.0. BTN = clamp(wtnA + wtnB - modifier, 0, min(wtnA, wtnB) + 5). Assert both WTN values are finite numbers; throw descriptive error if not. Zero-BTN pairs excluded by caller. TDD. Skills: /typescript-unit-testing. Acceptance: Known WTN/hop inputs produce expected BTN values; non-numeric WTN throws error; boundary cases (BTN=0, cap applied) verified.

title: US3-G: SQL writer (sql-writer.ts)
description: Implement tools/galaxy-seeder/src/sql-writer.ts to generate batched INSERT SQL wrapped in BEGIN/COMMIT transaction. Batch sizes: star_systems 500, routes 500, trade_pairs 1000. Dedicated escapeSQL function handling single quotes, NUL bytes. JSON columns normalized via JSON.stringify then SQL-escaped. No RAISE() guard (red-team finding: rely on UNIQUE constraints). TDD with adversarial inputs (O'Brien's Star, nested JSON with quotes). Skills: /typescript-unit-testing, /security-review. Acceptance: Generated SQL is valid; string escaping handles all special characters; adversarial names don't break SQL.

title: US3-H: Seeder CLI entry point (index.ts)
description: Implement tools/galaxy-seeder/src/index.ts CLI with --input (generator output dir), --output (SQL file path), --verbose flags. Orchestrates: read → validate → build graph → compute BTN → write SQL. TDD following tools/galaxy-generator/src/index.spec.ts pattern for CLI arg parsing and error cases. Skills: /typescript-unit-testing. Acceptance: CLI produces valid SQL file from fixture input; missing args show usage; validation errors exit with clear message.

title: US3-I: End-to-end integration test
description: Integration test: generate SQL from fixture data (10 star systems, 5 routes), apply to D1, query via all three repositories. Verify: row counts, field values, bidirectional route queries, BTN ordering, prefix search with LIKE escaping. Fixture includes adversarial system name (O'Brien's Star) and pre-computed expected trade pairs. Skills: /vitest-integration-testing, /d1-repository-implementation. Acceptance: Full pipeline from fixture JSON → SQL → D1 → repository queries produces correct results for all user stories.

title: US1-A: StarSystemRepository port interface
description: Define StarSystemRepository interface in src/application/ports/ with findById(id) and findByName(name) methods returning StarSystem | null. Also add searchByNamePrefix(prefix, limit?) returning StarSystem[]. JSDoc required per code standards. Skills: /prefactoring, /glossary, /ddd-domain-modeling. Acceptance: Interface compiles with strict TypeScript; explicit return types; JSDoc on all methods.

title: US2-A: RouteRepository port interface
description: Define RouteRepository interface in src/application/ports/ with findConnectedSystems(systemId) returning array of connected systems with route costs. Skills: /prefactoring, /glossary, /ddd-domain-modeling. Acceptance: Interface compiles with strict TypeScript; explicit return types; JSDoc on all methods.

title: US4-A: TradePairRepository port interface
description: Define TradePairRepository interface in src/application/ports/ with findTradePartners(systemId) returning partner systems with BTN values ordered by descending BTN. Skills: /prefactoring, /glossary, /ddd-domain-modeling. Acceptance: Interface compiles with strict TypeScript; explicit return types; JSDoc on all methods.

title: US1-B: D1 row mappers for galaxy entities
description: Create src/infrastructure/galaxy/mappers.ts with mapRowToStarSystem, mapRowToRoute, mapRowToTradePair functions. JSON columns (density, attributes, planetary, civilization, trade_codes, economics) parsed with parseJsonColumn helper that wraps JSON.parse in try/catch with descriptive error (system ID + column name). Boolean mapping: is_oikumene === 1. TDD: test with valid rows and malformed JSON. Skills: /d1-repository-implementation, /error-handling-patterns. Acceptance: All mapper functions convert D1 rows to domain types correctly; malformed JSON produces descriptive error.

title: US1-C: D1StarSystemRepository implementation
description: Implement D1StarSystemRepository in src/infrastructure/galaxy/ with findById, findByName, searchByNamePrefix. Prefix search must escape LIKE metacharacters (%, _, \) and use ESCAPE clause. Default limit of 50 for prefix search. Minimum prefix length of 2 characters. TDD with @cloudflare/vitest-pool-workers isolatedStorage. Skills: /d1-repository-implementation, /typescript-unit-testing. Acceptance: US1 scenarios pass — query by ID returns full record; non-existent ID returns null. US5 scenarios pass — exact name match works; prefix search returns matching systems; LIKE wildcards in prefix are escaped.

title: US2-B: D1RouteRepository implementation
description: Implement D1RouteRepository in src/infrastructure/galaxy/ with findConnectedSystems. Use UNION ALL for bidirectional lookup (origin_id query UNION ALL destination_id query) for index efficiency per red-team findings. TDD with @cloudflare/vitest-pool-workers isolatedStorage. Skills: /d1-repository-implementation, /typescript-unit-testing. Acceptance: US2 scenarios pass — connected systems from hub returns all neighbors with costs; system with no routes returns empty list.

title: US4-B: D1TradePairRepository implementation
description: Implement D1TradePairRepository in src/infrastructure/galaxy/ with findTradePartners. Use UNION ALL for bidirectional lookup. Results ordered by BTN descending. TDD with @cloudflare/vitest-pool-workers isolatedStorage. Skills: /d1-repository-implementation, /typescript-unit-testing. Acceptance: US4 scenarios pass — trade partners returned with BTN values ordered descending; isolated system returns empty list.

title: US1-D: Wire galaxy repositories into ServiceFactory
description: Modify src/di/serviceFactory.ts to add lazy-initialized getters for StarSystemRepository, RouteRepository, and TradePairRepository, following existing wiring pattern. Skills: /clean-architecture-validator. Acceptance: All three repositories accessible via ServiceFactory; dependency injection follows existing pattern.

description: Security review finding (MEDIUM/operational): Several jq patterns in beads-tasks SKILL.md, batch-patterns.md, and jq-cookbook.md still use .[] instead of .issues[] when processing br list --json output. title: [sp:09-review] Code review for ralph-automation
description: Review implemented code for quality, security, and adherence to spec requirements

title: Fix lock file race condition in ralph.sh
description:
**Code Review Finding**

- **Category**: quality
- **File**: ralph.sh:185-212
- **Severity**: Medium

## Description

The lock file check and creation are not atomic. A TOCTOU (time-of-check-time-of-use) race condition exists between checking if the lock file exists and creating it.

## Risk

Two concurrent executions could both pass the check before either creates the lock, allowing concurrent runs despite the locking mechanism.

## Recommended Fix

Use `flock` or atomic file creation with `noclobber` option:
```bash
set -o noclobber
echo $$ > "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null || { echo "Lock exists"; exit 1; }
```

---

_Duplicate Key: ralph.sh:185:quality_
close_reason: Used atomic file creation with noclobber to prevent TOCTOU race condition

title: Improve PID validation in lock file check
description:
**Code Review Finding**

- **Category**: quality
- **File**: ralph.sh:179-181
- **Severity**: Medium

## Description

`kill -0 "$pid"` only checks if a process exists, not if it is actually a ralph.sh instance. PID reuse could lead to false positives.

## Risk

A stale lock file with a reused PID (now belonging to an unrelated process) would block legitimate execution of ralph.sh.

## Recommended Fix

Store process name or command in lock file and verify both PID and process identity:
```bash
is_ralph_process() {
    local pid="$1"
    kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null && \
        [[ $(ps -p "$pid" -o comm= 2>/dev/null) == *ralph* ]]
}
```

---

_Duplicate Key: ralph.sh:179:quality_
close_reason: Added is_ralph_running() that validates both PID existence and process identity

**Acceptance**: 
- Template includes wrangler.toml even though source repo has none
- wrangler.toml contains name = "{{ app_name }}"
- wrangler.toml contains pages_build_output_dir = "hugo/public"
- Contains commented-out D1, KV, R2 binding placeholders
close_reason: All subtasks complete: wrangler.toml with app_name, pages_build_output_dir, and D1/KV/R2 bindings integrated into build script

title: US3: Instantiate Template into New Project
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-template-builder/spec.md §US-3
**Goal**: Enable automatic tmplr installation and template instantiation
**Acceptance**: 
- just install-tmplr downloads correct binary for platform (macOS/Linux, x64/arm64)
- Verifies SHA256 checksum before installation
- tmplr make substitutes {{ app_name }} in all template files
- Instantiated project passes npm install && npm run check
close_reason: All subtasks complete: install-tmplr.sh downloads, verifies, and installs tmplr v0.0.9 for macOS/Linux

title: Create install-tmplr.sh with platform and architecture detection
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-template-builder/spec.md §US-3, FR-014 through FR-016, research.md §Platform Detection Strategy
**Skills**: None (Bash script)
**Files**: scripts/install-tmplr.sh
**Acceptance**: 
- Detects OS via uname -s (Darwin, Linux)
- Detects architecture via uname -m (x86_64, arm64, aarch64)
- Maps to correct binary name (tmplr-{arch}-{platform})
- Reports unsupported OS/arch with clear error message
close_reason: Created install-tmplr.sh with platform/arch detection using uname -s and uname -m

title: Implement tmplr binary download from GitHub releases
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-template-builder/spec.md §US-3, FR-014, FR-018, research.md §tmplr Binary Releases
**Skills**: None (Bash script)
**Files**: scripts/install-tmplr.sh
**Acceptance**: 
- Downloads from https://github.com/exlee/tmplr/releases/download/v0.0.9/
- Uses pinned version v0.0.9 (FR-018)
- Downloads to temporary location first
- Handles download failures gracefully
close_reason: Implemented download from GitHub releases v0.0.9 using curl/wget with error handling

title: Implement SHA256 checksum verification for downloaded binary
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-template-builder/spec.md §US-3, FR-020, data-model.md §Checksum Record
**Skills**: None (Bash script)
**Files**: scripts/install-tmplr.sh
**Acceptance**: 
- Embeds SHA256 checksums for all 4 platform binaries
- Uses sha256sum (Linux) or shasum -a 256 (macOS)
- Deletes file and exits if checksum mismatch
- Reports expected vs actual hash on failure
close_reason: Implemented SHA256 checksum verification with embedded checksums for all 4 platform binaries

title: Install binary to ~/.local/bin and make executable
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-template-builder/spec.md §US-3, FR-016, research.md §tmplr Installation Location
**Skills**: None (Bash script)
**Files**: scripts/install-tmplr.sh
**Acceptance**: 
- Creates ~/.local/bin if it does not exist
- Moves verified binary to ~/.local/bin/tmplr
- Makes binary executable (chmod +x)
- Skips download if already installed with correct version
close_reason: Installs to ~/.local/bin/tmplr, creates dir if needed, makes executable, skips if version matches

comments.text: Review complete: verified all 20 FRs pass. build-template.sh produces valid template (643 files, 1.3MB compressed). install-tmplr.sh has correct platform detection, SHA256 verification, and version pinning. Both scripts are syntactically valid bash. No blocking issues found.
